'''大小橋命'''（おおばせのみこと）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]・'''[[中臣氏]]'''の遠祖。
== 概要 == [[摂津国]][[東成郡]]小橋に居したとされ、墓所は同郡岡邑にあるとされ<ref>[[鈴木真年]]「中臣氏総系」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref><ref>[[中田憲信]]「荒木家系譜」『諸系譜』第三十三冊。</ref>、[[御勝山古墳]]がその奥津城とされる。
[[彦湯稲荷神社]]の祭神として祀られる。
== 系譜 == 父は[[中臣烏賊津|雷大臣命]]、母は[[武内宿禰]]の娘である忍媛命で、[[依網男垂見]]の娘である厳志媛を娶り、[[中臣阿麻毘舎卿|阿麻毘舎卿連]]と呉公の祖・[[毛呂古連]]を生んでいる。また葛城古奈咩を娶って民直の祖・[[冨子連]]を生んでいる。同母弟に[[壱岐氏|伊岐宿禰]]の祖・[[真根子命]]と志斐連、表連の祖・[[弟子命]]が、異母弟に美乃直、栗原連の祖・[[日本大臣命]]がいる<ref>鈴木真年「中臣氏総系」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref><ref>中田憲信「荒木家系譜」『諸系譜』第三十三冊。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[卜部]] *[[占部氏]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおおばせのみこと}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:中臣氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

'''大小橋命'''（おおばせのみこと）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]・'''[[中臣氏]]'''の遠祖。
== 概要 == [[摂津国]][[東成郡]]小橋に居したとされ、墓所は同郡岡邑にあるとされ<ref>[[鈴木真年]]「中臣氏総系」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref><ref>[[中田憲信]]「荒木家系譜」『諸系譜』第三十三冊。</ref>、[[御勝山古墳]]がその奥津城とされる。
[[彦湯稲荷神社]]や[[比売許曽神社]]の祭神として祀られる。彦湯稲荷神社の社伝では[[天児屋根命]]の十三世の後胤で、[[成務天皇|成務朝]]に社地の味原郷で誕生したとされ、父は[[中臣烏賊津|中臣雷大臣]]、母は紀氏清夫とし、三人兄弟の長男とされる。また比売許曽神社の近くに大小橋命の胞衣を納めた胞衣塚がある。
== 系譜 == 父は[[中臣烏賊津|雷大臣命]]、母は[[武内宿禰]]の娘である忍媛命で、[[依網男垂見]]の娘である厳志媛を娶り、[[中臣阿麻毘舎卿|阿麻毘舎卿連]]と呉公の祖・[[毛呂古連]]を生んでいる。また葛城古奈咩を娶って民直の祖・[[冨子連]]を生んでいる。同母弟に[[壱岐氏|伊岐宿禰]]の祖・[[真根子命]]と志斐連、表連の祖・[[弟子命]]が、異母弟に美乃直、栗原連の祖・[[日本大臣命]]がいる<ref>鈴木真年「中臣氏総系」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref><ref>中田憲信「荒木家系譜」『諸系譜』第三十三冊。</ref>。

[[彦湯稲荷神社]]や[[比売許曽神社]]の祭神として祀られる。彦湯稲荷神社の社伝では[[天児屋根命]]の十三世の後胤で、[[成務天皇|成務朝]]に社地の味原郷で誕生したとされ、父は[[中臣烏賊津|中臣雷大臣]]、母は紀氏清夫とし、三人兄弟の長男とされる。また比売許曽神社の近くに大小橋命の胞衣を納めた胞衣塚がある。
== 系譜 == 父は[[中臣烏賊津|雷大臣命]]、母は[[武内宿禰]]の娘である忍媛命で、[[依網男垂見]]の娘である厳志媛を娶り、[[中臣阿麻毘舎卿|阿麻毘舎卿連]]と呉公の祖・[[毛呂古連]]を生んでいる。また葛城古奈咩を娶って民直の祖・[[冨子連]]を生んでいる。同母弟に[[壱岐氏|伊岐宿禰]]の祖・[[真根子命]]と志斐連、表連の祖・[[弟子命]]が、異母弟に美乃直、栗原連の祖・[[日本大臣命]]がいる<ref>鈴木真年「中臣氏総系」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref><ref>中田憲信「荒木家系譜」『諸系譜』第三十三冊。</ref>。

'''大小橋命'''（おおばせのみこと）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]・'''[[中臣氏]]'''の祖。中臣鎌足の9世祖父であるとされる。
== 概要 == [[摂津国]][[東成郡]]小橋に居したとされ、墓所は同郡岡邑にあるとされ<ref>[[鈴木真年]]「中臣氏総系」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref><ref>[[中田憲信]]「荒木家系譜」『諸系譜』第三十三冊。</ref>、[[御勝山古墳]]がその奥津城とされる。
[[彦湯稲荷神社]]や[[比売許曽神社]]の祭神として祀られる。彦湯稲荷神社の社伝では[[天児屋根命]]の十三世の後胤で、[[成務天皇|成務朝]]に社地の味原郷で誕生したとされ、父は[[中臣烏賊津|中臣雷大臣]]、母は紀氏清夫とし、三人兄弟の長男とされる。また比売許曽神社の近くに大小橋命の胞衣を納めた胞衣塚がある。
== 系譜 == 父は[[中臣烏賊津|雷大臣命]]、母は[[武内宿禰]]の娘である忍媛命で、子に[[中臣阿麻毘舎卿|阿麻毘舎卿連]]がいる。同母弟に[[壱岐氏|伊岐宿禰]]の祖・[[真根子命]]が、異母弟に美乃直、栗原連の祖・[[日本大臣命]]がいる<ref>鈴木真年「中臣氏総系」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref><ref>中田憲信「荒木家系譜」『諸系譜』第三十三冊。</ref>。

{{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命}}{{ill|Ōhashi-no-Mikoto|ja|大小橋命}}O-o-obase-no-mikoto (大小橋命) {{Infobox noble | name = Ōbashi no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = [[Emperor Seimu|Reign of Emperor Seimu]]? | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | ranks = | ruler = | clan = [[Nakatomi clan]] | parents = Father: [[Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto]] | siblings = [[Maneko no Mikoto]]<ref name="#1">[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]</ref>, [[Magatsuko no Mikoto]], [[Nihon Ōmi no Mikoto]] | spouse = | children = [[Nakatomi no Amabisha-kyō|Amabisha-kyō Ren no Kimi]] | notable_achievements = }}
'''Ōbashi no Mikoto''' (おおばせのみこと) was an influential figure of the [[Kofun period]] and an ancestor of the '''[[Nakatomi clan]]'''.
== Overview == Ōbashi no Mikoto is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Ubuyu Inari Shrine|ja|産湯稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売許曽神社}}. According to the Ubuyu Inari Shrine tradition, he was a 13th-generation descendant of [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]] and was born during the reign of [[Emperor Seimu]] in the Ajihara district where the shrine is located. His father was {{ill|Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|中臣雷大臣}}, and his mother is identified as Kishi Kiyoo. He is said to have been the eldest of three brothers. Near Himekoso Shrine, there is a "Hōei Mound," where his placenta is said to have been buried.
== Genealogy == Ōbashi no Mikoto's father was {{ill|Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|中臣雷大臣}}, and his son was {{ill|Nakatomi no Amabisha-kyō|ja|中臣阿麻毘舎卿連}}. His full brother was [[Maneko no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the {{ill|Iki no Sukune clan|ja|壱岐宿禰}}, and his half-brother was [[Nihon Ōmi no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the Nakatomi Kurihara clan<ref name="#1"/>.

{{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命}}{{ill|Ōhashi-no-Mikoto|ja|大小橋命}}O-o-obase-no-mikoto (大小橋命) {{Infobox noble | name = Ōbashi no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = [[Emperor Seimu|Reign of Emperor Seimu]]? | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | ranks = | ruler = | clan = [[Nakatomi clan]] | parents = Father: [[Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto]] | siblings = [[Maneko no Mikoto]]<ref name="#1">[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]</ref>, [[Magatsuko no Mikoto]], [[Nihon Ōmi no Mikoto]] | spouse = | children = [[Nakatomi no Amabisha-kyō|Amabisha-kyō Ren no Kimi]] | notable_achievements = }}
'''Ōbashi no Mikoto''' (おおばせのみこと) was an influential figure of the [[Kofun period]] and an ancestor of the '''[[Nakatomi clan]]'''.
== Overview == Ōbashi no Mikoto is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Ubuyu Inari Shrine|ja|産湯稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売許曽神社}}. According to the Ubuyu Inari Shrine tradition, he was a 13th-generation descendant of [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]] and was born during the reign of [[Emperor Seimu]] in the Ajihara district where the shrine is located. His father was {{ill|Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|中臣雷大臣}}, and his mother is identified as Kishi Kiyoo. He is said to have been the eldest of three brothers. Near Himekoso Shrine, there is a "Hōei Mound," where his placenta is said to have been buried.
== Genealogy == Ōbashi no Mikoto's father was {{ill|Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|中臣雷大臣}}, and his son was {{ill|Nakatomi no Amabisha-kyō|ja|中臣阿麻毘舎卿連}}. His full brother was [[Maneko no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the {{ill|Iki no Sukune clan|ja|壱岐宿禰}}, and his half-brother was [[Nihon Ōmi no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the Nakatomi Kurihara clan<ref name="#1"/>.

{{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命}}{{ill|Ōhashi-no-Mikoto|ja|大小橋命}}O-o-obase-no-mikoto (大小橋命) {{Infobox noble | name = Ōbashi no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = [[Emperor Seimu|Reign of Emperor Seimu]]? | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | ranks = | ruler = | clan = [[Nakatomi clan]] | parents = Father: [[Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto]] | siblings = [[Maneko no Mikoto]]<ref name="#1">[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]</ref>, [[Magatsuko no Mikoto]], [[Nihon Ōmi no Mikoto]] | spouse = | children = [[Nakatomi no Amabisha-kyō|Amabisha-kyō Ren no Kimi]] | notable_achievements = }}
'''Ōbashi no Mikoto''' (おおばせのみこと) was an influential figure of the [[Kofun period]] and an ancestor of the '''[[Nakatomi clan]]'''.
== Overview == Ōbashi no Mikoto is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Ubuyu Inari Shrine|ja|産湯稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売許曽神社}}. According to the Ubuyu Inari Shrine tradition, he was a 13th-generation descendant of [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]] and was born during the reign of [[Emperor Seimu]] in the Ajihara district where the shrine is located. His father was {{ill|Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|中臣雷大臣}}, and his mother is identified as Kishi Kiyoo. He is said to have been the eldest of three brothers. Near Himekoso Shrine, there is a "Hōei Mound," where his placenta is said to have been buried.
== Genealogy == Ōbashi no Mikoto's father was {{ill|Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|中臣雷大臣}}, and his son was {{ill|Nakatomi no Amabisha-kyō|ja|中臣阿麻毘舎卿連}}. His full brother was [[Maneko no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the {{ill|Iki no Sukune clan|ja|壱岐宿禰}}, and his half-brother was [[Nihon Ōmi no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the Nakatomi Kurihara clan<ref name="#1"/>.
== References == {{Reflist}}

{{ill|Ōkogashi no Mikoto|ja|大小橋命}}{{ill|Ōhashi-no-Mikoto|ja|大小橋命}}O-o-obase-no-mikoto (大小橋命) {{Infobox noble | name = Ōbashi no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = [[Emperor Seimu|Reign of Emperor Seimu]]? | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | ranks = | ruler = | clan = [[Nakatomi clan]] | parents = Father: [[Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto]] | siblings = [[Maneko no Mikoto]]<ref name="#1">[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]</ref>, [[Magatsuko no Mikoto]], [[Nihon Ōmi no Mikoto]] | spouse = | children = [[Nakatomi no Amabisha-kyō|Amabisha-kyō Ren no Kimi]] | notable_achievements = }}
'''Ōbashi no Mikoto''' (おおばせのみこと) was an influential figure of the [[Kofun period]] and an ancestor of the '''[[Nakatomi clan]]'''.
== Overview == Ōbashi no Mikoto is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Ubuyu Inari Shrine|ja|産湯稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売許曽神社}}. According to the Ubuyu Inari Shrine tradition, he was a 13th-generation descendant of [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]] and was born during the reign of [[Emperor Seimu]] in the Ajihara district where the shrine is located. His father was {{ill|Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|中臣雷大臣}}, and his mother is identified as Kishi Kiyoo. He is said to have been the eldest of three brothers. Near Himekoso Shrine, there is a "Hōei Mound," where his placenta is said to have been buried.
== Genealogy == Ōbashi no Mikoto's father was {{ill|Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|中臣雷大臣}}, and his son was {{ill|Nakatomi no Amabisha-kyō|ja|中臣阿麻毘舎卿連}}. His full brother was [[Maneko no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the {{ill|Iki no Sukune clan|ja|壱岐宿禰}}, and his half-brother was [[Nihon Ōmi no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the Nakatomi Kurihara clan<ref name="#1"/>.

'''Ōbashi no Mikoto''' (おおばせのみこと) was an influential figure of the [[Kofun period]] and an ancestor of the '''[[Nakatomi clan]]'''.
== Overview == Ōbashi no Mikoto is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Ubuyu Inari Shrine|ja|産湯稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売許曽神社}}. According to the Ubuyu Inari Shrine tradition, he was a 13th-generation descendant of [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]] and was born during the reign of [[Emperor Seimu]] in the Ajihara district where the shrine is located. His father was {{ill|Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|中臣雷大臣}}, and his mother is identified as Kishi Kiyoo. He is said to have been the eldest of three brothers. Near Himekoso Shrine, there is a "Hōei Mound," where his placenta is said to have been buried.
== Genealogy == Ōbashi no Mikoto's father was {{ill|Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|中臣雷大臣}}, and his son was {{ill|Nakatomi no Amabisha-kyō|ja|中臣阿麻毘舎卿連}}. His full brother was [[Maneko no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the {{ill|Iki no Sukune clan|ja|壱岐宿禰}}, and his half-brother was [[Nihon Ōmi no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the Nakatomi Kurihara clan<ref name="#1"/>.

'''Ōbashi no Mikoto''' (おおばせのみこと) was an influential figure of the [[Kofun period]] and an ancestor of the '''[[Nakatomi clan]]'''.
== Overview == Ōbashi no Mikoto is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Ubuyu Inari Shrine|ja|産湯稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売許曽神社}}. According to the Ubuyu Inari Shrine tradition, he was a 13th-generation descendant of [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]] and was born during the reign of [[Emperor Seimu]] in the Ajihara district where the shrine is located. His father was {{ill|Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|中臣雷大臣}}, and his mother is identified as Kishi Kiyoo. He is said to have been the eldest of three brothers. Near Himekoso Shrine, there is a "Hōei Mound," where his placenta is said to have been buried.
== Genealogy == Ōbashi no Mikoto's father was {{ill|Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|中臣雷大臣}}, and his son was {{ill|Nakatomi no Amabisha-kyō|ja|中臣阿麻毘舎卿連}}. His full brother was [[Maneko no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the {{ill|Iki no Sukune clan|ja|壱岐宿禰}}, and his half-brother was [[Nihon Ōmi no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the Nakatomi Kurihara clan<ref name="#1"/>.

'''Ōbashi no Mikoto''' (おおばせのみこと) was an influential figure of the [[Kofun period]] and an ancestor of the '''[[Nakatomi clan]]'''.
== Overview == Ōbashi no Mikoto is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Ubuyu Inari Shrine|ja|産湯稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売許曽神社}}. According to the Ubuyu Inari Shrine tradition, he was a 13th-generation descendant of [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]] and was born during the reign of [[Emperor Seimu]] in the Ajihara district where the shrine is located. His father was {{ill|Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|中臣雷大臣}}, and his mother is identified as Kishi Kiyoo. He is said to have been the eldest of three brothers. Near Himekoso Shrine, there is a "Hōei Mound," where his placenta is said to have been buried.
== Genealogy == Ōbashi no Mikoto's father was {{ill|Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|中臣雷大臣}}, and his son was {{ill|Nakatomi no Amabisha-kyō|ja|中臣阿麻毘舎卿連}}. His full brother was [[Maneko no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the {{ill|Iki no Sukune clan|ja|壱岐宿禰}}, and his half-brother was [[Nihon Ōmi no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the Nakatomi Kurihara clan<ref name="#1"/>.

'''Ōbashi no Mikoto''' (おおばせのみこと) was an influential figure of the [[Kofun period]] and an ancestor of the '''[[Nakatomi clan]]'''.
== Overview == Ōbashi no Mikoto is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Ubuyu Inari Shrine|ja|産湯稲荷神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売許曽神社|12=draft}}. According to the Ubuyu Inari Shrine tradition, he was a 13th-generation descendant of [[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]] and was born during the reign of [[Emperor Seimu]] in the Ajihara district where the shrine is located. His father was {{ill|Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|中臣雷大臣|12=draft}}, and his mother is identified as Kishi Kiyoo. He is said to have been the eldest of three brothers. Near Himekoso Shrine, there is a "Hōei Mound," where his placenta is said to have been buried.
== Genealogy == Ōbashi no Mikoto's father was {{ill|Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|中臣雷大臣|12=draft}}, and his son was {{ill|Nakatomi no Amabisha-kyō|ja|中臣阿麻毘舎卿連|12=draft}}. His full brother was [[Maneko no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the {{ill|Iki no Sukune clan|ja|壱岐宿禰|12=draft}}, and his half-brother was [[Nihon Ōmi no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the Nakatomi Kurihara clan<ref name="#1"/>.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of people before the Asuka period]] * [[:en:Radical_25|Urabe]] * [[Urabe clan]]

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 大小橋命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = [[成務天皇|成務朝]]？ | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = | 氏族 = [[中臣氏]] | 父母 = 父：[[雷大臣命]] | 兄弟 = 弟子命<ref name="#1">『[[新撰姓氏録]]』</ref>、[[真根子命]]、[[日本大臣命]] | 妻 = | 子 = [[中臣阿麻毘舎卿|阿麻毘舎卿連公]] | 特記事項 = }}
'''大小橋命'''（おおばせのみこと）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]・'''[[中臣氏]]'''の祖。
== 概要 == [[産湯稲荷神社]]や[[比売許曽神社]]の祭神として祀られる。産湯稲荷神社の社伝では[[天児屋根命]]の十三世の後胤で、[[成務天皇|成務朝]]に社地の味原郷で誕生したとされ、父は[[中臣烏賊津|中臣雷大臣]]、母は紀氏清夫とし、三人兄弟の長男とされる。また比売許曽神社の近くに大小橋命の胞衣を納めた胞衣塚がある。
== 系譜 == 父は[[中臣烏賊津|雷大臣命]]で、子に[[中臣阿麻毘舎卿|阿麻毘舎卿連]]がいる。同母弟に[[壱岐氏|伊岐宿禰]]の祖・[[真根子命]]が、異母弟に中臣栗原連の祖・[[日本大臣命]]がいる<ref name="#1"/>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] *[[卜部]] *[[占部氏]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおおばせのみこと}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:中臣氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大小橋命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Nakatomi no Amahisa|qid=Q97160710}}|father={{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|qid=Q55536814}}|mother={{ill|Q108616853|qid=Q108616853}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q99517235]] Wikidata name: Ōbase no Mikoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Ōbashi no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Reign of Emperor Seimu]]? | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | Ranks = | ruler = | clan = [[Nakatomi clan]] | parents = Father: [[Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto]] | siblings = [[Maneko no Mikoto]]<ref name="#1">[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]</ref>, [[Magatsuko no Mikoto]], [[Nihon Ōmi no Mikoto]] | spouse = | children = [[Nakatomi no Amabisha-kyō|Amabisha-kyō Ren no Kimi]] | notable_achievements = }}
'''Ōbashi no Mikoto''' (おおばせのみこと) was an influential figure of the [[Kofun period]] and an ancestor of the '''[[Nakatomi clan]]'''.
== Overview == Ōbashi no Mikoto is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Ubuyu Inari Shrine|ja|産湯稲荷神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubuyu Inari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売許曽神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso Shrine|qq=}}. According to the Ubuyu Inari Shrine tradition, he was a 13th-generation descendant of [[Ame-no-Koyane]] and was born during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] in the Ajihara district where the shrine is located. His father was {{ill|Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|中臣雷大臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and his mother is identified as Kishi Kiyoo. He is said to have been the eldest of three brothers. Near Himekoso Shrine, there is a "Hōei Mound," where his placenta is said to have been buried.
== Genealogy == Ōbashi no Mikoto's father was {{ill|Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|中臣雷大臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and his son was {{ill|Nakatomi no Amabisha-kyō|ja|中臣阿麻毘舎卿連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Amabisha-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491615}}. His full brother was [[Maneko no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the {{ill|Iki no Sukune clan|ja|壱岐宿禰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki no Sukune clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and his half-brother was [[Nihon Ōmi no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the Nakatomi Kurihara clan<ref name="#1"/>.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of people before the Asuka period]] * [[:en:Radical_25|Urabe]] * [[Urabe clan]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大小橋命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Nakatomi no Amahisa|WD=Q97160710}}|father={{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|WD=Q55536814}}|mother={{ill|Q108616853|WD=Q108616853}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q99517235]] Wikidata name: Ōbase no Mikoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Ōbashi no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Reign of Emperor Seimu]]? | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | Ranks = | ruler = | clan = [[Nakatomi clan]] | parents = Father: [[Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto]] | siblings = [[Maneko no Mikoto]]<ref name="#1">[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]</ref>, [[Magatsuko no Mikoto]], [[Nihon Ōmi no Mikoto]] | spouse = | children = [[Nakatomi no Amabisha-kyō|Amabisha-kyō Ren no Kimi]] | notable_achievements = }}
'''Ōbashi no Mikoto''' (おおばせのみこと) was an influential figure of the [[Kofun period]] and an ancestor of the '''[[Nakatomi clan]]'''.
== Overview == Ōbashi no Mikoto is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Ubuyu Inari Shrine|ja|産湯稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubuyu Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売許曽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso Shrine|qq=}}. According to the Ubuyu Inari Shrine tradition, he was a 13th-generation descendant of [[Ame-no-Koyane]] and was born during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] in the Ajihara district where the shrine is located. His father was {{ill|Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|中臣雷大臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and his mother is identified as Kishi Kiyoo. He is said to have been the eldest of three brothers. Near Himekoso Shrine, there is a "Hōei Mound," where his placenta is said to have been buried.
== Genealogy == Ōbashi no Mikoto's father was {{ill|Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|中臣雷大臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and his son was {{ill|Nakatomi no Amabisha-kyō|ja|中臣阿麻毘舎卿連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Amabisha-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491615}}. His full brother was [[Maneko no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the {{ill|Iki no Sukune clan|ja|壱岐宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki no Sukune clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and his half-brother was [[Nihon Ōmi no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the Nakatomi Kurihara clan<ref name="#1"/>.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of people before the Asuka period]] * [[:en:Radical_25|Urabe]] * [[Urabe clan]]

'''Ōbashi no Mikoto''' (おおばせのみこと) was an influential figure of the [[Kofun period]] and an ancestor of the '''[[Nakatomi clan]]'''.
== Overview == Ōbashi no Mikoto is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Ubuyu Inari Shrine|ja|産湯稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubuyu Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売許曽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso Shrine|qq=}}. According to the Ubuyu Inari Shrine tradition, he was a 13th-generation descendant of [[Ame-no-Koyane]] and was born during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}} in the Ajihara district where the shrine is located. His father was {{ill|Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|中臣雷大臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and his mother is identified as Kishi Kiyoo. He is said to have been the eldest of three brothers. Near Himekoso Shrine, there is a "Hōei Mound," where his placenta is said to have been buried.
== Genealogy == Ōbashi no Mikoto's father was {{ill|Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|中臣雷大臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and his son was {{ill|Nakatomi no Amabisha-kyō|ja|中臣阿麻毘舎卿連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Amabisha-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491615}}. His full brother was [[Maneko no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the {{ill|Iki no Sukune clan|ja|壱岐宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki no Sukune clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and his half-brother was [[Nihon Ōmi no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the Nakatomi Kurihara clan<ref name="#1"/>.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of people before the Asuka period]] * {{ill|Radical_25|en|Radical_25|WD=Q94724|lt=Urabe}} * [[Urabe clan]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大小橋命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大小橋命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大小橋命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Nakatomi no Amahisa|WD=Q97160710|1=Nakatomi no Amahisa}}|father={{ill|Nakatomi no Ikatsu|WD=Q55536814|1=Nakatomi no Ikatsu}}|mother={{ill|Q108616853|WD=Q108616853|1=Oshihime no Mikoto}}}} {{Infobox noble | name = Ōbashi no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Reign of Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}}? | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | religious_name = | burial_place = | Ranks = | ruler = | clan = [[Nakatomi clan]] | parents = Father: [[Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto]] | siblings = [[Maneko no Mikoto]]<ref name="#1">{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}</ref>, [[Magatsuko no Mikoto]], [[Nihon Ōmi no Mikoto]] | spouse = | children = [[Nakatomi no Amabisha-kyō|Amabisha-kyō Ren no Kimi]] | notable_achievements = }}
'''Ōbashi no Mikoto''' (おおばせのみこと) was an influential figure of the [[Kofun period]] and an ancestor of the '''[[Nakatomi clan]]'''.
== Overview == Ōbashi no Mikoto is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Ubuyu Inari Shrine|ja|産湯稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubuyu Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Himekoso Shrine|ja|比売許曽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himekoso Shrine|qq=}}. According to the Ubuyu Inari Shrine tradition, he was a 13th-generation descendant of [[Ame-no-Koyane]] and was born during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}} in the Ajihara district where the shrine is located. His father was {{ill|Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|中臣雷大臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and his mother is identified as Kishi Kiyoo. He is said to have been the eldest of three brothers. Near Himekoso Shrine, there is a "Hōei Mound," where his placenta is said to have been buried.
== Genealogy == Ōbashi no Mikoto's father was {{ill|Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja|中臣雷大臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikadzuchi Ōmi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and his son was {{ill|Nakatomi no Amabisha-kyō|ja|中臣阿麻毘舎卿連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Amabisha-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491615|1=Q135491615}}. His full brother was [[Maneko no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the {{ill|Iki no Sukune clan|ja|壱岐宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki no Sukune clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and his half-brother was [[Nihon Ōmi no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the Nakatomi Kurihara clan<ref name="#1"/>.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of people before the Asuka period]] * {{ill|Radical_25|en|Radical_25|WD=Q94724|lt=Urabe|1=Radical 25}} * [[Urabe clan]]
